title: Examples
intro: Real onboarding scenarios with intent, execution steps, and expected outcomes.

scenarios.title: Add new endpoint safely
scenarios.goal: Ship a new API operation without drifting Angular/backend types.
scenarios.context: Use this when product adds a new API capability and both frontend/backend teams work in parallel.
steps: Add endpoint shape to the contract file with request and response statuses.
Run generation and wire Angular client calls + backend handler stubs.
Execute compatibility checks and review report before merge.
expected: Generated artifacts include the new endpoint types.
Frontend can consume the endpoint without manual DTO authoring.
No unexpected breaking changes appear in check output.

scenarios.title: Validate contract changes in CI
scenarios.goal: Detect breaking API changes before release.
scenarios.context: Use this in pull requests when multiple teams modify contract files frequently.
steps: Validate schema format as a fast first gate.
Generate or compare summaries against baseline in CI.
Fail pipeline automatically when breaking changes are detected.
expected: Invalid contracts fail fast before deployment stages.
Breaking changes are visible in machine-readable output.
Release decisions are based on explicit compatibility signals.

commonPitfalls: Committing contract changes without regenerated artifacts.
Running `check` only locally and skipping CI enforcement.
Treating generated code as hand-edited source.
Not documenting expected status code payloads in contract responses.
